name: conductor-validator
description: Validates Conductor project artifacts for completeness,
capabilities: Check if Conductor root directory exists and list contents
Verify presence of required project files (index.md, product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md, tracks.md)
Display all track directories and their status markers
Validate task progress markers in plan.md files
Confirm track ID naming convention compliance
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill uses bash commands exclusively for read-only file system checks (ls -la, ls). The external_commands risk is minimal because all commands are hardcoded directory listings with no user input injection vectors. No network access, code execution, or file modification capabilities present.
valueStatement: Conductor projects require consistent artifact validation across setup and implementation phases. This skill automates directory checks and progress marker verification to ensure project completeness.
userTitle: Validate Conductor project structure and progress
limitations: Only performs read-only validation, cannot fix missing files
Requires Conductor project structure to follow expected directory layout
Cannot validate file content correctness, only existence
Limited to local file system checks without remote validation

useCases.title: Post-Setup Validation
useCases.description: After initializing a new Conductor project, verify all required artifacts are present and properly structured before beginning implementation work.

useCases.title: Diagnostic Troubleshooting
useCases.description: When experiencing workflow issues or missing context, quickly audit the project structure to identify gaps in documentation or track organization.

useCases.title: Pre-Implementation Review
useCases.description: Before starting new feature tracks, confirm the project context is complete and all prerequisite documentation exists for informed decision-making.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Directory Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if the conductor directory exists and list its contents to verify the project structure is in place.

promptTemplates.title: Required Files Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify all required Conductor files exist: index.md, product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md, and tracks.md. Report any missing files.

promptTemplates.title: Track Progress Review
promptTemplates.prompt: List all tracks in the conductor/tracks directory and check their status markers in tracks.md. Summarize which tracks are not started, in progress, or complete.

promptTemplates.title: Full Project Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a complete Conductor project validation: check directory structure, verify required files, review track status markers, and validate track ID naming conventions. Provide a summary report of any issues found.

bestPractices: Run validation after initial project setup to confirm all artifacts are created correctly
Check status markers in tracks.md regularly to maintain accurate progress tracking
Follow the track ID naming convention: type_name_YYYYMMDD for consistent organization
antiPatterns: Do not use this skill to modify or create files - it is read-only validation only
Avoid running validation on non-Conductor projects - results will be inaccurate
Do not ignore missing file warnings - address gaps before proceeding with implementation

faq.question: What files does this skill check for?
faq.answer: The skill verifies the presence of index.md, product.md, tech-stack.md, workflow.md, and tracks.md in the conductor directory root.

faq.question: Can this skill fix missing files?
faq.answer: No, this skill only performs read-only validation. It identifies missing files but does not create or modify them.

faq.question: What do the status markers mean in tracks.md?
faq.answer: Status markers indicate track progress: [ ] means not started, [~] means in progress, and [x] means complete.

faq.question: What is the correct track ID format?
faq.answer: Track IDs should follow the pattern: type_name_YYYYMMDD (for example: feature_user_auth_20250115).

faq.question: When should I run this validation?
faq.answer: Run validation after project setup, when diagnosing issues, or before starting implementation to verify project context is complete.

faq.question: Does this skill work with all AI tools?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, using Read, Glob, Grep, and Bash tools.
